babyDevelopment: A protective, waxy coating called vernix caseosa is forming over the baby's skin to protect it from the amniotic fluid. Sensory development is peaking—brain areas for smell, taste, hearing, vision, and touch are being established.
bodyChanges: You may experience 'round ligament pain'—sharp, short pains in your lower abdomen as the uterus grows. Your skin might become more sensitive to sunlight, so wear sunscreen to prevent melasma.
tips: Stay hydrated and maintain a high-fiber diet to prevent constipation
Practice relaxation techniques like deep breathing
Discuss your anatomy scan results with your doctor

travelAdvisory.note: Stay active and comfortable during travels.

nutrition: Omega-3 fatty acids (walnuts, salmon if cooked)
milestoneDescription: Vernix caseosa is forming to protect baby's delicate skin from amniotic fluid.
